channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Prosecutors release video of armed man storming correspondents' dinner
item.description: Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities say a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner and attempt to kill President Donald Trump.
item.content:encoded:
Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities say a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner and attempt to kill President Donald Trump.

(Image credit: Department of Justice)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump gives the go-ahead for a major new Canada-U.S. oil pipeline
item.description: More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.
item.content:encoded:
More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Texas coastal city faces worsening water shortage due to prolonged drought
item.description: After years of drought, Corpus Christi, Texas, is on the brink of a water emergency, as freshwater sources have dried up, leaving close to half a million people at risk of not having drinking water.
item.content:encoded: After years of drought, Corpus Christi, Texas, is on the brink of a water emergency, as freshwater sources have dried up, leaving close to half a million people at risk of not having drinking water.
item.dc:creator: Lucio Vasquez

item.title: Taxpayers might pay millions more to fight wildfires as jet fuel skyrockets
item.description: The Iran war has nearly doubled jet fuel prices in the United States. That means the bill for firefighting aircraft operations this summer will likely rise by tens of millions of dollars.
item.content:encoded: The Iran war has nearly doubled jet fuel prices in the United States. That means the bill for firefighting aircraft operations this summer will likely rise by tens of millions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Austin Amestoy

item.title: Trump pulls Casey Means' stalled surgeon general nomination, announces new pick
item.description: President Trump says he's nominating former Fox News Channel contributor Dr. Nicole Saphier for surgeon general after Dr. Casey Means' path forward stalled in the Senate over questions about her experience and her stance on vaccines.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump says he's nominating former Fox News Channel contributor Dr. Nicole Saphier for surgeon general after Dr. Casey Means' path forward stalled in the Senate over questions about her experience and her stance on vaccines.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The Onion's bid to take over Infowars moves to the Texas Supreme Court
item.description: The state's highest court will now consider a deal that would allow the Onion to license the Infowars brand name and turn the show into a mockery of itself.
item.content:encoded:
The state's highest court will now consider a deal that would allow the Onion to license the Infowars brand name and turn the show into a mockery of itself.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: The Iraq vet redefining mindfulness, one bourbon at a time
item.description: Like many vets, it took Fred Minnick time to find the best way to cope with what he would learn was PTSD. For Minnick, sense and peace came with bourbon — and "taste mindfulness."
item.content:encoded:
Bottom Shelf: How a Forgotten Brand of Bourbon Saved One Man's Life.'/>

Like many vets, it took Fred Minnick time to find the best way to cope with what he would learn was PTSD. For Minnick, sense and peace came with bourbon — and "taste mindfulness."

(Image credit: Dustin Franz for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: After lackluster tour turnout, Turning Point wraps things up with excited crowd in Idaho
item.description: The final stop on Turning Point USA's college campus tour at the University of Idaho seemed more like the organization's previous events, with audience member debates and an energetic, young crowd.
item.content:encoded:
The final stop on Turning Point USA's college campus tour at the University of Idaho seemed more like the organization's previous events, with audience member debates and an energetic, young crowd.

(Image credit: Saige Miller)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: After clashing with lawmakers over the Iran war, Pete Hegseth returns for second day of testimony, a look at Fed chair Jerome Powell's legacy, SCOTUS strikes severe blow to Voting Rights Act.
item.content:encoded: After clashing with lawmakers over the Iran war, Pete Hegseth returns for second day of testimony, a look at Fed chair Jerome Powell's legacy, SCOTUS strikes severe blow to Voting Rights Act.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Long naval deployments take toll on military families
item.description: The impacts of long deployments in the Middle East and the Caribbean are causing an increasing strain on military families in Navy towns like Norfolk, Virginia.
item.content:encoded: The impacts of long deployments in the Middle East and the Caribbean are causing an increasing strain on military families in Navy towns like Norfolk, Virginia.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh
